title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: ReproStat provides tools diagnosing reproducibility statistical modeling workflows. central idea perturb data small ways (bootstrap, subsampling, noise) measure much model outputs change across perturbations. Stable outputs imply high reproducibility; volatile outputs flag potential concerns. package computes: Coefficient stability – variance estimates across perturbations. P-value stability – frequency significant findings. Selection stability – frequency variable selection. Prediction stability – variance predictions. Reproducibility Index (RI) – composite 0–100 score.

title: Introduction to ReproStat
code: mt_diag <- run_diagnostics(   mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   data   = mtcars,   B      = 200,   method = "bootstrap" ) mt_diag #> ReproStat Diagnostics #> --------------------- #> Formula   : mpg ~ wt + hp + disp  #> Backend   : lm  #> Method    : bootstrap  #> Iterations: 200  #> Terms     : (Intercept), wt, hp, disp

title: Introduction to ReproStat
code: coef_stability(mt_diag) #>  (Intercept)           wt           hp         disp  #> 6.709592e+00 1.040656e+00 1.229663e-04 7.452477e-05 pvalue_stability(mt_diag) #>    wt    hp  disp  #> 0.905 0.840 0.015 selection_stability(mt_diag) #>    wt    hp  disp  #> 1.000 1.000 0.515 prediction_stability(mt_diag)$mean_variance #> [1] 0.9650964

title: Introduction to ReproStat
code: reproducibility_index(mt_diag) #> $index #> [1] 88.77472 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9188745  0.8200000  0.8383333  0.9737808

title: Introduction to ReproStat
code: oldpar <- par(mfrow = c(1, 2)) plot_stability(mt_diag, "coefficient") plot_stability(mt_diag, "selection") par(oldpar)

title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: Three perturbation strategies supported:
code: # Bootstrap resampling (default) run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, method = "bootstrap")  # Subsampling (80 % of rows without replacement) run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, method = "subsample", frac = 0.8)  # Gaussian noise injection (5 % of each column's SD) run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, method = "noise", noise_sd = 0.05)

what: Model comparison with CV ranking stability
title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: Use cv_ranking_stability() compare multiple candidate models across repeated cross-validation runs.
code: models <- list(   baseline = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   compact  = mpg ~ wt + hp,   expanded = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec )  cv_result <- cv_ranking_stability(models, mtcars, v = 5, R = 40) cv_result$summary #>      model mean_rmse   sd_rmse mean_rank top1_frequency #> 1  compact  2.684893 0.1629443     1.050           0.95 #> 2 expanded  2.811187 0.1605096     2.425           0.05 #> 3 baseline  2.798253 0.1474168     2.525           0.00 plot_cv_stability(cv_result, metric = "top1_frequency")

title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: package dataset-agnostic. data frame numeric response numeric predictors works: datasets missing values, subset complete cases first:
code: iris_diag <- run_diagnostics(   Sepal.Length ~ Sepal.Width + Petal.Length + Petal.Width,   data   = iris,   B      = 150,   method = "noise",   noise_sd = 0.03 ) reproducibility_index(iris_diag) #> $index #> [1] 99.99019 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9996922  1.0000000  1.0000000  0.9999156 aq <- na.omit(airquality[, c("Ozone", "Solar.R", "Wind", "Temp")]) aq_diag <- run_diagnostics(   Ozone ~ Solar.R + Wind + Temp,   data   = aq,   B      = 150,   method = "bootstrap" ) reproducibility_index(aq_diag) #> $index #> [1] 89.77414 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.7181772  0.8888889  1.0000000  0.9838995

what: Uncertainty in the RI: bootstrap confidence interval
title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: ri_confidence_interval() resamples stored perturbation draws estimate uncertainty RI without refitting models.
code: set.seed(20260307) d <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, mtcars, B = 150) ri_confidence_interval(d, level = 0.95, R = 500) #>     2.5%    97.5%  #> 86.89235 90.23516

title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: ReproStat supports four backends. use API; backend argument changes.
code: # Generalized linear model (logistic) run_diagnostics(am ~ wt + hp, mtcars, B = 100,                 family = stats::binomial())  # Robust regression (requires MASS) if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE))   run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, mtcars, B = 100, backend = "rlm")  # LASSO (requires glmnet) if (requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE))   run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec, mtcars,                   B = 100, backend = "glmnet", en_alpha = 1)

title: Introduction to ReproStat
text: ggplot2 installed, plot_stability_gg() plot_cv_stability_gg() return ggplot objects can customised.
code: if (requireNamespace("ggplot2", quietly = TRUE)) {   set.seed(1)   d_gg <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, mtcars, B = 50)   print(plot_stability_gg(d_gg, "coefficient"))   print(plot_stability_gg(d_gg, "selection")) }

what: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Reproducibility Diagnostics Statistical Modeling ReproStat evaluates stable fitted model’s outputs (coefficients, p-values, variable selection, predictions) data perturbations. reports four stability metrics aggregates single Reproducibility Index (RI) 0–100 scale.

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
code: devtools::install("ReproStat")

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
code: library(ReproStat)  set.seed(1) diag <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 200)  # Composite reproducibility index reproducibility_index(diag) #> $index #> [1] 74.3 #> #> $components #>      coef    pvalue selection prediction #>    0.8021    0.7154    0.7412     0.6821  # Confidence interval for the RI ri_confidence_interval(diag, R = 500)

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
code: perturb_data()       (optional, standalone use)        | run_diagnostics()    <-- main entry point        |    reprostat object        |    ┌───┴────────────────────────────────────┐    │                                        │ stability metrics                  reproducibility_index() coef_stability()                         │ pvalue_stability()               ri_confidence_interval() selection_stability() prediction_stability()    │ plot_stability() cv_ranking_stability() / plot_cv_stability()

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Generates single perturbed copy data frame.
code: perturb_data(data, method = "bootstrap", frac = 0.8, noise_sd = 0.05) set.seed(1) d_boot <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "bootstrap") d_sub  <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "subsample", frac = 0.7) d_nois <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "noise",     noise_sd = 0.1)

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Fits base model re-fits B perturbed datasets, collecting coefficient estimates, p-values, predictions across iterations. Backends: Returns S3 object class "reprostat" containing coef_mat (B × p), p_mat (B × p), pred_mat (n × B), base_fit, y_train, metadata.
code: run_diagnostics(   formula,   data,   B           = 200,   method      = c("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"),   alpha       = 0.05,   frac        = 0.8,   noise_sd    = 0.05,   predict_newdata = NULL,   family      = NULL,   backend     = c("lm", "glm", "rlm", "glmnet"),   en_alpha    = 1,   lambda      = NULL ) set.seed(1)  # OLS d_lm  <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 200)  # Logistic regression d_glm <- run_diagnostics(am ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars, B = 200,                          family = binomial())  # Robust regression d_rlm <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 200,                          backend = "rlm")  # LASSO  (lambda chosen by cv.glmnet when lambda = NULL) d_las <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec, data = mtcars,                          B = 200, backend = "glmnet", en_alpha = 1)  # Ridge d_rid <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec, data = mtcars,                          B = 200, backend = "glmnet", en_alpha = 0)  # Elastic net (alpha = 0.5) d_en  <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec, data = mtcars,                          B = 200, backend = "glmnet", en_alpha = 0.5)  print(d_lm)

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: four functions accept "reprostat" object return scalar vector summaries. Lower variance / higher frequency values indicate greater stability.

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Per-coefficient variance across B iterations. Lower = stable.
code: coef_stability(d_lm) #>  (Intercept)          wt          hp #>     5.182741    0.043812    0.000231

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Proportion iterations coefficient significant alpha. Values near 0 1 stable; 0.5 indicates random decisions.
code: pvalue_stability(d_lm) #>  (Intercept)          wt          hp #>        0.972       0.958       0.731

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: pvalue_stability() excludes intercept. Useful assessing variable selection consistency.
code: selection_stability(d_lm) #>    wt    hp #> 0.958 0.731

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Pointwise prediction variance across iterations, plus mean.
code: ps <- prediction_stability(d_lm) ps$mean_variance          # scalar summary ps$pointwise_variance     # length-n vector

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Aggregates four stability components composite RI 0–100. Interpretation: Note: backend = "glmnet", p-values undefined, pvalue selection components NA RI averaged remaining two components (coef prediction).
code: ri <- reproducibility_index(d_lm) ri$index        # 0–100 scalar ri$components   # named vector: coef, pvalue, selection, prediction

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Bootstrap confidence interval RI, computed resampling already-stored perturbation draws (extra model fitting).
code: ri_confidence_interval(d_lm, level = 0.95, R = 1000) #>  2.5% 97.5% #>  68.4  79.9

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Bar chart histogram one stability dimension.
code: plot_stability(d_lm, type = "coefficient")   # coefficient variance plot_stability(d_lm, type = "pvalue")        # significance frequency plot_stability(d_lm, type = "selection")     # selection frequency plot_stability(d_lm, type = "prediction")    # prediction variance histogram

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Evaluates model selection stability running repeated K-fold cross-validation recording error-metric rank candidate model across repetitions. Logistic models (log-loss metric):
code: cv_ranking_stability(   formulas,   data,   v       = 5L,   R       = 30L,   seed    = 20260307L,   family  = NULL,   backend = c("lm", "glm", "rlm", "glmnet"),   en_alpha = 1,   lambda  = NULL,   metric  = c("auto", "rmse", "logloss") ) models <- list(   m1 = mpg ~ wt + hp,   m2 = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp,   m3 = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec )  cv_obj <- cv_ranking_stability(models, mtcars, v = 5, R = 50) cv_obj$summary #>   model mean_rmse sd_rmse mean_rank top1_frequency #> 1    m3     2.891   0.312      1.24           0.72 #> 2    m2     3.014   0.389      1.94           0.24 #> 3    m1     3.207   0.401      2.82           0.04 glm_models <- list(   m1 = am ~ wt + hp,   m2 = am ~ wt + hp + qsec ) cv_ranking_stability(glm_models, mtcars, v = 5, R = 30,                      family = binomial(), metric = "logloss")

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
text: Bar chart CV ranking results.
code: plot_cv_stability(cv_obj, metric = "top1_frequency")  # proportion ranked 1st plot_cv_stability(cv_obj, metric = "mean_rank")        # average rank

title: ReproStat — Reproducibility Diagnostics for Statistical Modeling
code: library(ReproStat) set.seed(42)  # 1. Run diagnostics diag <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp, data = mtcars, B = 200)  # 2. Inspect individual metrics coef_stability(diag) pvalue_stability(diag) selection_stability(diag) prediction_stability(diag)$mean_variance  # 3. Composite RI + CI ri <- reproducibility_index(diag) cat(sprintf("RI = %.1f\n", ri$index)) ri_confidence_interval(diag, R = 500)  # 4. Visualise par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) plot_stability(diag, "coefficient") plot_stability(diag, "pvalue") plot_stability(diag, "selection") plot_stability(diag, "prediction")  # 5. Cross-validation ranking stability models <- list(   base     = mpg ~ wt + hp,   extended = mpg ~ wt + hp + disp ) cv_obj <- cv_ranking_stability(models, mtcars, v = 5, R = 50) plot_cv_stability(cv_obj)

Cross-validation ranking stability model comparison visualization utilities also provided.

text: Computes variance regression coefficient across perturbation iterations. Lower variance indicates greater stability.

code: coef_stability(diag_obj)

text: diag_obj reprostat object run_diagnostics.

title: Coefficient stability — coef_stability
text: named numeric vector per-coefficient variances.

title: Coefficient stability — coef_stability
code: set.seed(1) d <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50) coef_stability(d) #>  (Intercept)           wt           hp  #> 3.512876e+00 3.919577e-01 4.783342e-05

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
text: Evaluates model selection stability repeatedly running \(K\)-fold cross-validation recording error-metric rank candidate model across repetitions.  Supports four modeling backends: "lm", "glm", "rlm" (robust regression via MASS), "glmnet" (penalized regression via glmnet).

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
code: cv_ranking_stability(   formulas,   data,   v = 5L,   R = 30L,   seed = 20260307L,   family = NULL,   backend = c("lm", "glm", "rlm", "glmnet"),   en_alpha = 1,   lambda = NULL,   metric = c("auto", "rmse", "logloss") )

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
text: formulas named list formulas, one per candidate model. data data frame. v Number cross-validation folds. Must satisfy \(2 \le v \le n\). Default 5. R Number cross-validation repetitions. Default 30. seed Integer random seed reproducibility. Default 20260307. family GLM family object (e.g. stats::binomial()) NULL (default) use stats::lm.  Used backend = "glm". backend Modeling backend.  One "lm" (default), "glm", "rlm", "glmnet". en_alpha Elastic-net mixing parameter glmnet (default 1 LASSO). Ignored backends. lambda Regularization parameter glmnet. NULL (default), selected per fold via glmnet::cv.glmnet.  Ignored backends. metric Error metric used ranking. One "auto" (default: "rmse" lm/"rlm"/"glmnet", "logloss" glm non-Gaussian family), "rmse", "logloss".  "logloss" metric assumes binary (0/1) response; defined multi-class outcomes.

title: Cross-validation ranking stability — cv_ranking_stability
text: list components: settings List v, R, seed,       metric, family, backend, en_alpha,       lambda. rmse_mat \(R \times M\) matrix per-repeat mean       error values (RMSE log-loss depending metric). rank_mat \(R \times M\) integer matrix per-repeat       model ranks (rank 1 = best). summary Data frame columns model,       mean_rmse, sd_rmse, mean_rank,       top1_frequency, ordered mean rank.  Note: columns       mean_rmse sd_rmse store mean standard       deviation chosen error metric (RMSE log-loss); column       names retained backwards compatibility.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
text: Generates perturbed version dataset using one three strategies: bootstrap resampling, subsampling without replacement, Gaussian noise injection.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
code: perturb_data(   data,   method = c("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"),   frac = 0.8,   noise_sd = 0.05,   response_col = NULL )

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
text: data data frame. method Character string specifying perturbation method. One "bootstrap" (default), "subsample", "noise". frac Fraction rows retain subsampling. Ignored methods. Must \((0, 1]\). Default 0.8. noise_sd Noise level fraction column's standard deviation. Ignored unless method = "noise". Default 0.05. response_col Optional character string naming response (outcome) column exclude noise injection.  Useful want perturb predictors leave outcome unchanged.  Ignored method = "bootstrap" method = "subsample".  NULL (default) numeric columns including response receive noise.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
text: data frame columns data. number   rows equals nrow(data) bootstrap noise,   floor(frac * nrow(data)) subsampling.

title: Perturb a dataset — perturb_data
code: set.seed(1) d_boot <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "bootstrap") d_sub  <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "subsample", frac = 0.7) d_nois <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "noise", noise_sd = 0.1)  # Perturb predictors only, leave the response (mpg) unchanged: d_pred_only <- perturb_data(mtcars, method = "noise",                             noise_sd = 0.1, response_col = "mpg")

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: Computes composite reproducibility index (RI) 0–100 scale aggregating normalized versions coefficient, p-value, selection, prediction stability.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
code: reproducibility_index(diag_obj)

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: diag_obj reprostat object run_diagnostics.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: list components: index Scalar RI 0–100 scale. components Named numeric vector four sub-scores:       coef, pvalue, selection, prediction.       pvalue NA backend = "glmnet";       selection always available.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
text: component mapped \([0, 1]\) follows: Coefficient component (\(C_\beta\)) \(C_\beta = \frac{1}{p}\sum_{j=1}^{p}     \exp\!\left(-S_{\beta,j} / (|\hat\beta_j^{(0)}| +     \bar\beta)\right)\),     \(\bar\beta = \max(\mathrm{median}_j|\hat\beta_j^{(0)}|,\, 10^{-4})\)     global scale reference.  prevents exponential     collapsing zero weakly-identified (near-zero) predictors.     Includes model terms (intercept predictors). P-value component (\(C_p\)) \(C_p = \frac{1}{p'}\sum_{j \neq \text{intercept}} |2 S_{p,j} - 1|\),     \(S_{p,j}\) proportion perturbation iterations     term \(j\) significant level alpha \(p'\)     number predictor terms.  Values near 0 1 (consistent     decisions) score high; 0.5 (random) scores zero.     NA backend = "glmnet". Selection component (\(C_\mathrm{sel}\)) "lm", "glm", "rlm": mean     sign consistency across predictors — proportion     perturbation iterations predictor's estimated sign     agrees base-fit sign.  Captures stability effect direction,     distinct significance stability (\(C_p\)).     "glmnet": mean non-zero selection frequency —     proportion perturbation iterations predictor's     coefficient non-zero.  Always available (never NA).     Excludes intercept. Prediction component (\(C_\mathrm{pred}\)) \(C_\mathrm{pred} = \exp(-\bar S_\mathrm{pred} /     (\mathrm{Var}(y) + \varepsilon))\).     Prediction variance relative outcome variance drives decay. RI \(100 \times (C_\beta + C_p + C_\mathrm{sel} + C_\mathrm{pred}) / k\), \(k\) number non-NA components.  backend = "glmnet", \(C_p\) NA RI based three components (\(k = 3\)): \(C_\beta\), \(C_\mathrm{sel}\), \(C_\mathrm{pred}\).  backends contribute four components (\(k = 4\)). Comparability across backends: "glmnet" uses three components "lm"/"glm"/"rlm" use four, RI values directly comparable across backends.

title: Reproducibility index — reproducibility_index
code: set.seed(1) d <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50) reproducibility_index(d) #> $index #> [1] 97.50225 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9562648  0.9600000  1.0000000  0.9838253  #>

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
text: Fits model original data, repeatedly fits perturbed versions collect coefficient estimates, p-values, predictions downstream stability analysis.  Four modeling backends supported: ordinary least squares ("lm"), generalized linear models ("glm"), robust regression ("rlm" via MASS), penalized regression ("glmnet" via glmnet).

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
code: run_diagnostics(   formula,   data,   B = 200,   method = c("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"),   alpha = 0.05,   frac = 0.8,   noise_sd = 0.05,   predict_newdata = NULL,   family = NULL,   backend = c("lm", "glm", "rlm", "glmnet"),   en_alpha = 1,   lambda = NULL,   perturb_response = FALSE )

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
text: formula model formula. data data frame. B Integer. Number perturbation iterations. Default 200. method Perturbation method passed perturb_data. One "bootstrap" (default), "subsample", "noise". alpha Significance threshold p-value selection stability. Default 0.05. frac Subsampling fraction. Passed perturb_data. Default 0.8. noise_sd Noise level. Passed perturb_data. Default 0.05. predict_newdata Optional data frame --sample prediction stability. Defaults data. family GLM family object (e.g. stats::binomial(), stats::poisson()). Used backend = "glm" (family non-NULL backend = "lm", case backend silently promoted "glm"). backend Modeling backend.  One "lm" (default), "glm", "rlm" (robust M-estimation via MASS::rlm), "glmnet" (penalized regression via glmnet::glmnet). en_alpha Elastic-net mixing parameter passed glmnet::glmnet: 1 (default) gives LASSO, 0 gives ridge, intermediate values give elastic net. Ignored backends. lambda Regularization parameter glmnet.  NULL (default) penalty selected glmnet::cv.glmnet using lambda.min.  Ignored backends. perturb_response Logical. FALSE (default) method = "noise", noise added predictor columns; response variable left unchanged.  Set TRUE also perturb response (e.g. simulate measurement error outcome). effect method = "bootstrap" "subsample", row-level resampling naturally carries response along.

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
text: object class "reprostat", list components: call matched call. formula model formula. B Number iterations used. alpha Significance threshold used. base_fit Model fitted original data. y_train Response vector original data (used       internally RI computation). coef_mat B x p matrix coefficient estimates. p_mat B x p matrix p-values, -NA       backend = "glmnet" (p-values defined). pred_mat n x B matrix predictions. method Perturbation method used. family GLM family used, NULL. backend Backend used. en_alpha Elastic-net mixing parameter used (relevant       backend = "glmnet", otherwise NA).

title: Run reproducibility diagnostics — run_diagnostics
code: set.seed(1) # Linear model (small B for a quick check) diag_lm <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 20) print(diag_lm) #> ReproStat Diagnostics #> --------------------- #> Formula   : mpg ~ wt + hp  #> Backend   : lm  #> Method    : bootstrap  #> Iterations: 20  #> Terms     : (Intercept), wt, hp   # \donttest{ # Logistic regression diag_glm <- run_diagnostics(am ~ wt + hp + qsec, data = mtcars, B = 50,                             family = stats::binomial()) reproducibility_index(diag_glm) #> $index #> [1] 75.00492 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.2497427  1.0000000  0.8533333  0.8971209  #>   # Robust regression (requires MASS) if (requireNamespace("MASS", quietly = TRUE)) {   diag_rlm <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp, data = mtcars, B = 50,                               backend = "rlm")   reproducibility_index(diag_rlm) } #> $index #> [1] 96.84662 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.9190617  0.9800000  1.0000000  0.9748032  #>   # Penalized regression / LASSO (requires glmnet) if (requireNamespace("glmnet", quietly = TRUE)) {   diag_glmnet <- run_diagnostics(mpg ~ wt + hp + disp + qsec, data = mtcars,                                  B = 50, backend = "glmnet", en_alpha = 1)   reproducibility_index(diag_glmnet) } #> $index #> [1] 82.57931 #>  #> $components #>       coef     pvalue  selection prediction  #>  0.7604751         NA  0.7450000  0.9719042  #>  # }

title: ReproStat 0.1.0
text: run_diagnostics(): main entry point supporting "lm", "glm", "rlm" (via MASS), "glmnet" (via glmnet) backends; three perturbation methods ("bootstrap", "subsample", "noise"). New argument perturb_response (default FALSE) controls whether response column perturbed noise method. perturb_data(): standalone data perturbation bootstrap, subsampling, Gaussian noise injection. New argument response_col allows response column excluded noise perturbation. coef_stability(): variance coefficient estimates across perturbation iterations. pvalue_stability(): proportion iterations predictor significant; intercept excluded output. selection_stability(): sign consistency estimated coefficients "lm" / "glm" / "rlm" backends; non-zero selection frequency "glmnet" backend. Intercept excluded. genuinely distinct measure pvalue_stability(). prediction_stability(): pointwise prediction variance across perturbation iterations. Coefficient component (c_beta) now uses global scale reference (median(|base_coef|)) instead hard-coded epsilon, preventing score collapsing near-zero coefficients. c_p (p-value stability) c_sel (selection stability) now genuinely distinct components; previously computed quantity. backend = "glmnet", selection component (c_sel) now mean non-zero selection frequency always available (previously NA). RI glmnet therefore based three components instead two. ri_confidence_interval(): bootstrap confidence interval RI. seed argument now defaults NULL, leaving caller’s global RNG state undisturbed. Pass integer fix seed explicitly. cv_ranking_stability(): repeated K-fold CV ranking stability model comparison across four backends. plot_stability(), plot_cv_stability(): base-graphics visualisations. plot_stability_gg(), plot_cv_stability_gg(): optional ggplot2-based equivalents (require ggplot2).
